info.title: ReleaseCraft CLI
info.description: Command-line interface for ReleaseCraft - generate beautiful release notes from GitHub pull requests. Install as a global .NET tool and use from any directory.

options.name: --owner
options.description:
GitHub repository owner (username or organization)
Can be set in .releasecraftrc.json config file

options.name: --token
options.description:
GitHub personal access token (PAT)
If not provided, reads from GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file path (if not specified, writes to console)

options.name: --format
options.description:
Output format for release notes
Values: markdown (default), json, html, csv
Example: --format json

options.name: --audience
options.description:
Target audience mode for tone control
Values: client (non-technical), internal (technical, default)
Example: --audience client

options.name: --ai
options.description:
Enable AI-powered summarization and enhancement
Requires OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable

options.name: --tone
options.description:
AI tone style for generated content (requires --ai)
Values: professional (default), casual, marketing, technical
Example: --tone marketing

options.name: --help
options.description:
Show this help message
Configuration File:
ReleaseCraft looks for .releasecraftrc.json in:
1. Current directory (./.releasecraftrc.json)
2. Repository root (if in git repo)
3. User home directory (~/.releasecraftrc.json)
Example .releasecraftrc.json:
{
"owner": "microsoft",
"repo": "vscode",
"outputPath": "RELEASE_NOTES.md",
"defaultCategory": "Other Changes",
"ai": {
"enabled": true,
"apiKey": "env:OPENAI_API_KEY",
"model": "gpt-4-turbo",
"tone": "professional",
"features": {
"summarization": true,
"toneControl": true
}
}
}
Note: CLI arguments always override config file values.
